problem.title: Recover Binary Search Tree
problem.description: You are given the root of a binary search tree (BST), where the values of exactly two nodes of the tree were swapped by mistake.

example.explanation: Nodes 1 and 3 are swapped. In-order traversal detects two inversions; swap those two nodes.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Inorder Traversal [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: An inorder traversal of a BST produces a sorted sequence; swapping two nodes creates at most two positions where the current node's value is less than the previous node's value. The first such violation marks the first swapped node, and the last violation marks the second; swapping their values restores the BST.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class TreeNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.left = left
#         self.right = right

def recoverTree(root: Optional[object]) -> None:
    first = second = prev = None

    def inorder(node: Optional[object]) -> None:
        nonlocal first, second, prev
        if not node:
            return
        inorder(node.left)
        if prev and prev.val > node.val:
            if not first:
                first = prev
                second = node
            else:
                second = node
        prev = node
        inorder(node.right)

    inorder(root)
    if first and second:
        first.val, second.val = second.val, first.val
